filter_pushdown.index_action: skip blocks where block.max < 8766 OR block.min >= 9131; data sorted by l_shipdate so ~514/600 blocks skipped
secondary prune on surviving shipdate blocks: skip if block.max_discount < 500 OR block.min_discount > 700
combined with discount prune above: skip if block.min_quantity >= 2400

data_structures.aggregation: thread_local_int64_sum_array[nthreads] — scalar partial sums, no hash table needed; merge with simple loop

zone_map_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600] then [int32_t min, int32_t max, uint32_t count] x600 (12 bytes/block)
[uint32_t num_blocks=600] then [int32_t min1(discount), int32_t max1(discount), int32_t min2(qty), int32_t max2(qty), uint32_t count] x600 (20 bytes/block)
zone_map_usage.applied_after: zone_map_l_shipdate prune — further eliminates surviving blocks

physical_plan.scan_strategy: single_pass_fused — all 4 columns read together per qualifying block; predicate evaluation order: l_shipdate first (cheapest, most selective after zone map), then l_discount, then l_quantity; accumulate l_extendedprice * l_discount on pass

parallelism.strategy: morsel_driven — partition surviving blocks among threads via atomic fetch_add on block index counter
parallelism.morsel_unit: full blocks (100,000 rows each); zone-map alive block list pre-computed sequentially, then distributed to threads
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_partial_sum — each thread accumulates int64_t local_sum; final merge is sequential sum of 64 values — zero contention during hot scan loop
parallelism.memory_pattern: sequential_within_block — columns are contiguous binary arrays; each block is 100K * 8 bytes = 800KB per column, fitting well across thread L3 shares

index_usage.purpose: skip_blocks_outside_date_range — primary pruning; since lineitem is physically sorted by l_shipdate, this is extremely effective
secondary_skip_on_discount_and_quantity — applied to blocks surviving shipdate filter

physical_plan.output_scaling: final result = thread_sum_total / (scale_factor_price * scale_factor_discount) = total / (2 * 2) = total / 4; or keep as scaled int64 and convert at output
physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential — no random access; each surviving block read left-to-right across 4 column files

notes: lineitem sorted by l_shipdate → zone_map_l_shipdate prunes ~514/600 blocks (~86% skip), leaving ~86 blocks (~8.6M rows to scan)
zone_map_l_discount_qty gives secondary pruning on those 86 blocks
No hash table build, no join, no sort — pure scan+filter+sum pipeline
64 threads process ~86 blocks → ~1-2 blocks per thread, minimal overhead
Target: match or beat Umbra (14ms) / DuckDB (20ms); current GenDB at 93ms likely due to missing zone-map pruning and/or single-threaded scan
HDD note: data likely cached in OS page cache for repeated benchmarks; if cold read, I/O dominates and zone-map savings are critical
